rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for structural assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, and branching logic should use nested if-then-else structures.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features and ports at their respective scope levels.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use AGREE logical operators: `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`.
Constants and equations should use valid scalar types: `int`, `real`, `bool`.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly defined as data ports in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
